mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 4 Series engine typically costs £2,800–£6,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], with the volume B47 diesel at £2,800–£4,500 and the B58 petrol at £3,800–£6,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 4 Series engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 straight-six — the most dependable modern BMW engine in our dataset. The B48B20 petrol and B47D20A diesel are also excellent. Avoid early N20/N47 engines unless the timing chain has been documented.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 4 Series engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but a pre-2016 N20B20 or N47D20C without a documented timing-chain replacement is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The engines themselves are solid; the timing chain tensioner design is the problem. A 2016+ B47/B48/B58 has no such caveat.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my 4 Series timing chain is worn?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle for 1–2 seconds is the classic early warning, sometimes with a chain-noise change under load. On the N20, listen for a high-pitched whine at startup. On the N47, the rattle is from the rear of the engine. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £2,800–£5,500 engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends entirely on generation and bodystyle. Against a £25,000 M440i, a £5,000 B58 recon is just 20% of the car's value — ✅ safe. Against a £12,000 F32 440i, a £4,500 B58 recon is 38% — ⚠️ watch. Against a £8,000 early F32 N47, a £4,500 recon is 56% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 4 Series should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: 2017+ F32 LCI 440i with the B58 — RWD, 326 bhp, depreciated, and the chain risk is gone. For the newest experience, G22 M440i xDrive with 374 bhp and mild-hybrid. For practicality, F36 Gran Coupé or G26 Gran Coupé. Avoid pre-2016 N20/N47 cars without documented chain history.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 4 Series engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation, bodystyle, model year and variant (420i, 430d, M440i) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW 4 Series vs 3 Series — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they share engines across the same eras — the N20/N47/B48/B58 story is identical. The 4 Series is lower and wider, but the powertrains are the same. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The 4 Series offers more style; the 3 Series offers more practicality.

hero.tagPill: BMW 4 Series • 2014–Present • 2 Generations (F32/F33/F36, G22/G23/G26) • N20, N55, B48, B58, S55, S58
hero.h1: 4 Series Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW 4 Series engine across two generations and three bodystyles — from the N20 four-cylinder to the B58 straight-six and the S58 in the M4. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for every F32 to G26. Backed by 300+ real UK 4 Series engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The 4 Series rule — the 3 Series in a sharper suit: a B58 recon at £3,800–£6,000 against a £25,000 M440i is 15–24% of the car's value (✅ safe); the same engine in a high-mileage F32 440i at £12,000 is 32–50% (⚠️ watch). Run the maths per car, not per badge.
★★★★☆ — the B-family engines (B48, B58, B47) are the most dependable; the N20 and N47 carry the chain-tensioner caveat on early cars. The S58 in the M4 is proving robust.
B58B30 (440i/M440i) and B48B20 (430i) — the modern modular straight-six and four-cylinder
Pre-2016 N20B20 and N47D20C without documented timing-chain replacement — affects early F32/F33/F36 420i/428i and 418d/420d
2017+ F32 LCI with B58 (440i) or B48 (430i) — the B-series engines in a depreciated RWD coupe chassis
G22 M440i xDrive with B58 — 374 bhp, mild-hybrid, and the most refined 4 Series ever made
🏆 The Style-Led 3 Series — sportier, sharper, and rarer than its four-door sibling

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The 4 Series is the 3 Series with the roof cut off and the attitude turned up — and it shares the same engine story. The N20/N47 chain question defines the early cars; the B48/B58 defines the modern ones. Buy on the engine, not the bodystyle.

rows.value: H2: BMW 4 Series Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Two generations, three bodystyles, and one mission — to be the sportier, sharper alternative to the 3 Series. The right 4 Series depends on which generation and which engine you choose.
BMW 4 Series Coupé F32 🔥 Most Enquired — shared with F33/F36 [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.value: Our Verdict: The original 4 Series — lower, wider, and more aggressive than the 3 Series it replaced. Early N20/N47 cars carry the chain caveat; 2017+ LCI cars with B48/B58 are the sweet spot. The 440i with the B58 is a future classic.

rows.metric: Our Verdict: The open-top 4 Series — same engines as the F32, but with a folding hardtop that adds weight and complexity. The B58 in the 440i is the pick; the N20/N47 chain question applies equally. Buy the newest you can afford.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The four-door 4 Series — a liftback that combines coupe styling with hatchback practicality. Same engines as the F32/F33, but the F36's added practicality makes it the most sensible 4 Series. The 440i Gran Coupé is a genuine all-rounder.

Our Verdict: The second-generation 4 Series — CLAR platform, B-family engines only, and the divisive vertical kidney grille. The M440i xDrive with 374 bhp and mild-hybrid is the performance pick; the 430i with B48 is the sensible choice. No N-series engines means no chain caveat.

rows.metric: Our Verdict: The open-top G22 — fabric roof instead of the F33's folding hardtop, saving weight and complexity. Same B48/B58 engines. The M440i xDrive Convertible is the ultimate 4 Series for California dreaming in the UK.

rows.value: 2021–present • B48B20, B58B30 (M440i xDrive), plus the all-electric i4

Our Verdict: The second-generation Gran Coupé — the most practical 4 Series, now with an all-electric i4 sibling. The B58 in the M440i xDrive is the performance pick; the i4 is the future. The G26 is the 4 Series for people who actually need rear doors.
The F32 LCI — The Line That Matters
The 4 Series we recommend for used buyers

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 4 Series Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW 4 Series — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

engines.enquiries: — (pattern data — under B58 aggregate in source data)

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: B58B30 and B47D20A ratings: 90% confidence — high-volume modern engines with clear failure patterns and shared-platform data from the 3 Series. N20B20 and N47D20C ratings: 85% confidence — well-documented timing chain issues from BMW TSBs and specialist reports. N55B30 and N57D30 ratings: Expert-knowledge tier — documented as robust engines in the F32 era. S55B30 and S58B30 ratings: Pattern-data tier — M-car engines with proven track records but limited enquiry data. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The source data lists "4 Series" as a single entry, but the 4 Series is two generations across three bodystyles. The F32/F33/F36 and G22/G23/G26 share engines with the 3 Series (F30/G20). Enquiry figures for the 4 Series are partially aggregated with 3 Series data in the source file. Correction applied: The N20B20 timing chain issue affects early F32/F33/F36 420i and 428i models (2014–2016). This is the petrol equivalent of the N47 diesel chain problem — both were resolved with the B-series engine replacements in the 2017 LCI. Correction applied: The 4 Series LCI (2017) replaced the N20 with the B48 and the N55 with the B58. The B48/B58 are significantly more reliable than their N-series predecessors. Correction applied: The M4 (F82/F83) uses the S55 engine — a high-performance version of the N55. The G82/G83 M4 uses the S58 — a high-performance version of the B58. Both are robust but carry M-car servicing costs.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the 4 Series range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining early 4 Series issue — affects 2014–2016 F32/F33/F36 with N20 petrol and N47 diesel engines. Cold-start rattle is the warning; chain slip destroys the engine. £2,800–£5,500 recon [BMW-QUOTE].

problems.issue: N55 Oil Filter Housing & Cooling Leaks
problems.description: The N55's known weak points — oil filter housing gasket leaks and cooling system failures. Affects F32/F33/F36 435i (2014–2016). Repairs are £500–£1,200, not catastrophic if caught early.
The B-family engines' known weak points — coolant expansion tank leaks and water pump failures. Affects all B48/B58-powered 4 Series (2016+). £400–£800 repair, not catastrophic if caught early.

The B47 diesel's primary weak points — EGR cooler leaks and DPF clogging on urban-driven cars. Affects all B47-powered 4 Series (2015+). Well-documented, repairable, far less severe than the N47's chain issue.
Not an engine failure — but a common non-engine 4 Series enquiry. The folding hardtop on the F33 Convertible is complex and expensive to repair. Check operation before buying any F33.
The 3.0L six-cylinder diesel's known weak points — timing chain stretch and injector failure. Affects F32/F33/F36 430d/435d (2014–2019). Well-documented, repairable, but specialist-only work.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW 4 Series Market Intelligence — What 300+ UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — LCI B58 cars entering affordable range [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — newer models entering warranty-exit age [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬇ Falling as the parc ages out [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The 4 Series is effectively the 3 Series in a sharper suit — and it shares the same engine story. The N20/N47 chain question defines the early cars; the B48/B58 defines the modern ones. But the 4 Series attracts a different buyer — someone willing to pay a premium for style over practicality. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The F32/F33/F36 LCI (2017+) with B48/B58 engines are now entering affordable territory — £12,000–£18,000 for a 440i. These are the 4 Series to buy: the chain risk is gone, the B58 is bulletproof, and the depreciation has already done its work. (Editorial synthesis.)

The M4 (F82/F83 with S55, G82/G83 with S58) generates a different kind of enquiry — performance buyers researching upgrades or repairs, not everyday owners facing unexpected failures. The S55/S58 are proving robust, but M-car servicing costs are a different league. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW 4 Series Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one 4 Series based on this page, buy a 2017+ F32 LCI with the B58 (440i) or B48 (430i). The LCI brought the B-series engines that changed the reliability story — and on an early N20 or N47, a documented timing-chain inspection is worth more than any service stamp. The 440i Gran Coupé is the ultimate 4 Series — coupe style with hatchback practicality and the B58's bulletproof straight-six.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW 4 Series Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW 4 Series engine typically takes 8–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The 4 Series rule — the 3 Series in a sharper suit: A £3,800–£6,000 B58 recon against a £25,000 M440i sits at 15–24% of the car's value — ✅ safe. Against a £12,000 F32 440i, it's 32–50% — ⚠️ watch. And a £5,000–£10,000 S55 recon against a £20,000 M4 is 25–50% — still safe if the car is well-maintained, but a big bill. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Value the car first, then the engine.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a BMW 4 Series engine typically takes 8–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The 4 Series rule — the 3 Series in a sharper suit: A £3,800–£6,000 B58 recon against a £25,000 M440i sits at 15–24% of the car's value — ✅ safe. Against a £12,000 F32 440i, it's 32–50% — ⚠️ watch. And a £5,000–£10,000 S55 recon against a £20,000 M4 is 25–50% — still safe if the car is well-maintained, but a big bill. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Value the car first, then the engine.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW 4 Series Engine Range Evolved — 2014 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW spun the 3 Series Coupé/Convertible into the 4 Series. The N20/N47/N55/N57 were carryover engines from the 3 Series.
eras.worthKnowing: The N20/N47 timing chain issue defined this era — every early 4 Series diesel and 420i/428i lives in its shadow.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The LCI replaced the N20 and N47 with the B48 and B47, and the N55 with the B58 — the reliability story changed more than the styling.
eras.worthKnowing: The B58 440i is a future classic — 326 bhp, RWD, and the most dependable modern BMW straight-six.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The F82/F83 M4 used the S55 — a high-performance twin-turbo straight-six based on the N55.
eras.worthKnowing: The S55 is robust but has crank hub concerns on tuned cars. Standard cars are reliable.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The CLAR-platform G-series arrived — B-family engines only, mild-hybrid tech, and the divisive vertical grille.
eras.worthKnowing: The G22 M440i xDrive with 374 bhp is the most powerful non-M 4 Series ever. The S58 in the M4 is proving even more robust than the S55.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW 4 Series?

profiles.verdict: The F36 Gran Coupé is the most practical 4 Series — a liftback with real rear space. The G26 Gran Coupé is even better, with the i4 electric option. But if you genuinely need four doors regularly, the 3 Series Touring is the better choice.

The 4 Series is a great commuter car — comfortable, refined, and the B47/B48/B58 engines are efficient. The Gran Coupé is the most practical for daily use; the Coupé is for drivers who don't need rear doors.

A cheap early F32 N20/N47 is not a cheap 4 Series — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £15,000 F32 LCI 440i with B58 is the bargain; a £10,000 pre-LCI without chain paperwork is the trap.

The M440i with the B58 is the sweet spot — 374 bhp in the G22, 326 bhp in the F32 LCI. The M4 (F82/G82) is the full-fat experience, but the M440i offers 90% of the performance for half the running costs. Buy the F32 LCI 440i while values are still sensible.

The 4 Series is the style-led 3 Series — lower, wider, more aggressive. The G22's vertical grille is divisive but distinctive. The Gran Coupé offers the best blend of style and practicality. Buy the newest you can afford.

items.question: How much does a BMW 4 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW 4 Series engine typically costs £2,800–£6,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], with the volume B47 diesel at £2,800–£4,500 and the B58 petrol at £3,800–£6,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which BMW 4 Series engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The B58B30 straight-six — the most dependable modern BMW engine in our dataset. The B48B20 petrol and B47D20A diesel are also excellent. Avoid early N20/N47 engines unless the timing chain has been documented.

items.question: Which BMW 4 Series engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright — but a pre-2016 N20B20 or N47D20C without a documented timing-chain replacement is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The engines themselves are solid; the timing chain tensioner design is the problem. A 2016+ B47/B48/B58 has no such caveat.

items.question: How do I know if my 4 Series timing chain is worn?
items.answer: Cold-start rattle for 1–2 seconds is the classic early warning, sometimes with a chain-noise change under load. On the N20, listen for a high-pitched whine at startup. On the N47, the rattle is from the rear of the engine. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £2,800–£5,500 engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series engine?
items.answer: Depends entirely on generation and bodystyle. Against a £25,000 M440i, a £5,000 B58 recon is just 20% of the car's value — ✅ safe. Against a £12,000 F32 440i, a £4,500 B58 recon is 38% — ⚠️ watch. Against a £8,000 early F32 N47, a £4,500 recon is 56% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully.

items.question: Which BMW 4 Series should I buy?
items.answer: 2017+ F32 LCI 440i with the B58 — RWD, 326 bhp, depreciated, and the chain risk is gone. For the newest experience, G22 M440i xDrive with 374 bhp and mild-hybrid. For practicality, F36 Gran Coupé or G26 Gran Coupé. Avoid pre-2016 N20/N47 cars without documented chain history.
